description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a pitched roof made of reddish-orange clay tiles supported by sturdy wooden beams. The well features a wooden crank handle and spindle mechanism for drawing water, with a rope and bucket system visible above the well opening. The stonework is textured and irregular, giving a rustic appearance, and the well sits on a circular stone-paved platform. Several metal buckets are scattered around the base, and the wooden roof structure is robust, providing shelter over the well. The overall design is practical and visually reminiscent of classic wells found in parks or village squares.
description: A rustic stone water well with a tiled roof, wooden support beams, and a crank-operated bucket system, set on a circular stone platform.
description_view_0: The well is seen from an angle showing the stone base, wooden roof supports, tiled roof, crank handle, and several buckets on the ground.
description_view_1: This view highlights the full span of the tiled roof, the wooden crank mechanism, and the circular stone well opening.
description_view_2: The well is viewed from the opposite side, showing the stone base, roof, and a single bucket resting near the well.
description_view_3: A direct side view of the well, emphasizing the tiled roof, crank handle, and multiple buckets around the base.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden crank and spindle mechanism with a rope and bucket is present above the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low circular stone wall and covered by a pitched wooden and clay-tiled roof supported by wooden beams.
